records.ety: [L. devolvere, devolutum, to roll down; de + volvere to roll down; de + volvere to roll. See Voluble.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Devolved (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Devolving.]

defs: To roll onward or downward; to pass on.
quotes: Every headlong stream Devolves its winding waters to the main.

defs: To transfer from one person to another; to deliver over; to hand down; -- generally with upon, sometimes with to or into.
quotes: They devolved a considerable share of their power upon their favorite.
They devolved their whole authority into the hands of the council of sixty.

defs: To pass by transmission or succession; to be handed over or down; -- generally with on or upon, sometimes with to or into; as, after the general fell, the command devolved upon (or on) the next officer in rank.
quotes: His estate . . . devolved to Lord Somerville.
